summary: Juniperus rigida, the temple juniper, is a species of juniper, native to northern China, Mongolia, Korea, Japan, and the far southeast of Russia ( Sakhalin and Primorsky Krai ), occurring at altitudes of 10–2,200 metres (33–7,218 ft). The species is also naturalized in the United States ( California and Alabama ). It is closely related to Juniperus communis (common juniper) and Juniperus conferta (shore juniper), the latter sometimes treated as a variety or subspecies of J. rigida.
